title: I Got Tired Editing AI Slop — And I Finally Found a Way to Stop AI From Making Things Up
author: Matt Giaro
text:
I Got Tired Editing AI Slop — And I Finally Found a Way to Stop AI From Making Things Up
Now it knows all my ideas, my writing style, and helps me create content in minutes
I write with AI every day.
And yeah… I got tired of babysitting it.
Every time, it’s the same circus:
- Open a new chat.
- Paste writing samples.
- Give it context about what I’ve written before.
- Edit like crazy.
“No. That’s wrong. That’s not what I meant.”
Five versions later, I’m exhausted — sifting through thousands of words trying to stitch something together that doesn’t sound like a Wikipedia article written by a drunk baboon.
AI was supposed to save us time. Not to make us edit robot vomit.
But what if I told you that I finally found a solution to all of this?
Interested writing more content with AI that sounds like you?
I just finished a new free course you can grab on this link
The real reason why AI makes things up
That’s the part nobody tells you.
AI doesn’t hallucinate because you suck at prompting.
It hallucinates because it has not enough context. Every new chat is a blank slate. AI doesn’t know what you wrote last week. It doesn’t know your opinions, your stories, or your life experiences.
So it guesses. And guesses WRONG.
You can have the best prompts in the world. Won’t matter if AI has nothing real to pull from. It’s like hiring a ghostwriter and expecting it to write your biography without ever having met you.
So yeah, you don’t need better prompts. Nope.
You need to give AI access to:
- your actual ideas.
- your actual content.
- your actual methodologies.
ChatGPT and Claude Memory are expansive jokes
Some of you big brains might argue that ChatGPT and Claude got Memory.
Yeah, kinda.
But that memory is limited to what you say in the chats. Which means that it doesn’t have access to anything unless you create a new chat with it.
And if you ever used it, you know how bad it is at recalling details…
Another issue is the mix up: That in-house memory blends everything — your content, your 3am health worries, that embarrassing question you’d never ask out loud.
Now here is the ugly part: That memory is just a way to lock you in. If you want to switch from one AI to another, you’re basically locked into that AI that holds your memory.
You’re hostage to the chat/web version of ChatGPT, Claude, or Gemini.
You can’t build agents. You can’t switch to other interfaces.
Now, don’t get me talking about the “data export”. That stupid zip file is just a joke to meet regulations of people who never used AI anyways.
My dream solution had to give creators control over their data.
Your content makes AI dumber
Maybe you’ve tried like me stuffing all your content into a single chat.
Perhaps create a Claude project, or a CustomGPT.
The problem here are limitations. If you’re like me and have hundreds (or thousands) of pieces of content, you’ll hit the limits pretty darn fast.
Which means you need to wait until your usage resets or pay more.
The other big problem is that the bigger the context, the dumber the AI becomes.
Imagine trying to hold 100 books at once, and then I tell you to grab book number 7 and flip to page 312, line 12. You might be able to hold all the books, but good luck finding the exact line.
That’s what context-stuffing does.
Mega prompts kinda work (until they don’t)
Then I tried mega prompts with 2,000+ words.
Prompts that hold ALL your point of views on all the topics I cover. My brand philosophy. What I believe in. What I’m against. My stories.
Worked pretty decently. But these all require constant updates.
Every time I publish something new I had to manually update these files like a clerk.
The solution is pretty simple (on paper)
It’s to build a knowledge base that holds all your content in one place that you can connect to any AI, on any device.
Think of it as a Dropbox optimized for AI.
(You can’t use Dropbox or Google Drive out of the box, because these platforms are not built for AI. They stuff all your content with invisible data that clogs AI.)
So I went down that rabbit hole to find a solution.
Turns out, there wasn’t. So I tried to build this myself with no-code tools like n8n, pinecone, or Supabase.
But they all were complicated as hell:
- Nodes.
- API keys.
- Webhooks.
- JSON formatting.
I spent a few weeks trying to make this works with “free workflows” til I threw my hands in frustration.
The final fix
So I built Content Base myself.
The concept is simple: Every piece of content I’ve ever created lives in one place. It also syncs every new piece I publish automatically.
To hell with copy-paste. To hell with the ugly N8N or Make interface.
I click a button. Done.
Now I can connect my Content Base to ChatGPT and Claude. Or any other AI. Heck, I can even build agents who know ALL my content. Maybe a chatbot. Maybe an SEO bot. Maybe something else.
The point is this:
AI has access to my entire body of work which includes my frameworks, stories, and ideas.. Articles, newsletters, course transcripts, sales pages, video scripts — all are now instantly searchable in ChatGPT and Claude.
When I ask AI something, it looks at that base FIRST, only gets the relevant parts and brings it to the chat.
You don’t lose intelligence power because you only get the right info you need in that chat. You never run out of limits.
All this without the pain-in-the-butt manual work: No pasting. No reminding. No repeating myself like I’m talking to someone with Alzheimer’s.
AI retrieves what it needs. My ideas. My stories. My opinions. My voice.
It stops guessing because it finally has the right context to work with.
The result
I can now outline courses, blog posts, and books in minutes.
I simply ask Ai:
Outline a Substack article about topic Z using ideas in my content base. Make sure that each subheader is about one idea. Include relevant personal stories for each section.
I see what it comes up with, tweak it a bit. Then I tell it:
Now write the article by mimicking the writing style of the articles in my content base.
No more “that’s not what I meant.” No more correcting hallucinations. No more starting from scratch every single chat.
I prompt. AI pulls from my content. I enhance. I edit. Done.
This is what AI should’ve been from the start — not a dumb assistant you have to retrain every morning, but a tool that actually knows your work.
Most importantly, I can now also switch to AI models without having to start from scratch all the time.
I can now connect that same content base to AI agents, different AI models, etc.
Should you get your content base?
I realized that there are other creators who run into the same issue as I face, so I decided to make it a public app.
It ain’t cheap as Dropbox.
But if you value your time, and all it did it save you one hour on your next article it’ll already paid for itself for the next 12 months.
Click here if you want to take a look… or get my free ai-writing course instead.
